name: Fried Chicken Slices with Lemon Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the chicken skin and cut into 1 - 1/2 inch-wide, 2 inch-long thin slices.
2. For the marinade, mix 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon wine, 1 tablespoon light soy sauce, 1 tablespoon cornstarch, 1 tablespoon cold water, 1 egg yolk, and 1/8 teaspoon pepper. Marinate the chicken pieces for about 10 minutes.
3. For the coating, combine 6 tablespoons cornstarch and 3 tablespoons flour on a plate.
4. Heat oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to at least 350°F (175°C).
5. Deep fry the chicken slices until golden brown, about 1/2 minute. Remove from the hot oil and let cool for 10 seconds.
6. Reheat the oil to at least 375°F (190°C) and deep fry the chicken again for an additional 10 seconds. Drain on a wire rack or paper towels.
7. For the seasoning sauce, mix 3 tablespoons lemon juice, 3 tablespoons soup stock, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 2 teaspoons cornstarch, 1 teaspoon sesame oil, and 3 drops yellow food coloring in a small bowl until smooth.
8. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan over medium heat. Stir the sauce mixture until it boils and thickens. Add another tablespoon of hot oil to make the sauce glossy.
9. Pour the sauce over the fried chicken slices. Serve hot, garnished with sliced lemon and parsley. If desired, squeeze fresh lemon juice onto the chicken before serving for extra tanginess.
